capture.cameraPose: desktop preview with right-side operator panel and partial in-scene readable panels
capture.captureCommand: Chrome DevTools MCP take_screenshot after local Vite navigation

notes: The ED chest-pain case intent is visible, but the bay, actors, and equipment remain placeholder-level and should not be treated as clinical-environment realism evidence.
The visible actor uses primitive capsule geometry and the hand/input affordances are not realistic; this aligns with the current known gap from the worn-headset report.
The right-side operator text and trace buttons are readable in desktop preview, and the Quest Evidence panel is available lower in the control surface for copying runtime JSON.
The screenshot cannot prove immersive entry, hand-tracking locomotion, controller latency, or copy-to-clipboard behavior inside Quest Browser.
The in-scene text panels and actor body overlap in the desktop camera pose; adversarial reviewers should keep checking text occlusion, actor scale, and panel placement in headset runs.
This artifact is a desktop browser visual QA snapshot. It is not physical Quest foreground performance evidence and does not replace Patrick's worn-headset observations.
